sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The diamond pattern completion worked! The grid blocks are now all gray again (reset), and the cursor has moved - it's now at the LEFT end of the bar (the blue cursor is at the start). The right strip still has the green portion at the bottom.
So completing the diamond pattern:
1. Reset the grid to all gray
2. Moved the cursor back to the START of the bar
This is interesting - the cursor went from the END back to the START. This might be a "loop" behavior, or it could be that completing a pattern when the cursor is at the end wraps it back to the beginning.
Now I'm back to a state similar to the beginning, but with the right strip having some green. Let me think about what the goal might be:
- The cursor advances when I complete diamond patterns
- When it reaches the end, it wraps back to the start
- The right strip fills with green as I complete patterns
Maybe the goal is to fill the ENTIRE right strip with green? Currently only the bottom portion is green. Let me continue completing diamond patterns to advance the cursor again and see if more of the right strip fills.
Let me complete another diamond pattern.

sections.content:

# The right strip has green at bottom. Let me continue advancing.

</parameter>

Right strip partially green. Continuing patterns."}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Grid reset to gray, cursor at bar start. Right strip has green at bottom.
